blocks.content: Monday. 6:58am. The list was already up when we got there. A crowd of maybe thirty kids around a single sheet of paper on the main entrance door.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye. Don't run up there. Walk.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Because however it goes, you want to have walked up there. Not ran. Ya dig?
Jonathan found his name in four seconds. Of course he did — he knew exactly where to look. He didn't react, just stepped to the side to give me room.
I scanned the list top to bottom. Jenkins, L.
He let me have the thirty seconds. Didn't turn it into a moment, didn't make it loud. Just stood next to me while I looked at my own name on the wall and figured out what it meant.
